frameworks.name: dynamic gating
frameworks.one_line: Broadcast signal becomes effective only in neurons receiving a gating input

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation / evolving decision representation
frameworks.one_line: Analog graded evidence is preserved while the encoding population changes

frameworks.name: reference-frame remapping
frameworks.one_line: Intervening eye movements shift which neurons encode task-relevant signals

core_question: How can analog decision evidence be transferred between neural populations without rewiring synaptic connectivity?
key_assumptions: Decision evidence is represented as a graded analog variable
Synaptic connectivity is fixed across the transfer event
A gating signal selectively grants receptivity to a broadcast input
Intervening actions can trigger a switch in the encoding population
manipulations: Modelled smooth pursuit and saccadic eye movements as intervening actions
Compared transfer between different parietal cortex populations
Varied which subpopulation received the gating signal
key_findings: Graded information can be transferred between populations via gated receptivity to a broadcast signal
The model reproduces transfer of decision-related activity between parietal populations across eye movements
Continuous decision processes are maintained despite changes in frame of reference
interpretations: Dynamic gating provides a mechanism for flexible population reassignment without synaptic change
Widely broadcast signals plus selective receptivity explain experimentally observed cross-population transfer
